Home › How to claim the deduction

How to claim the deduction

You claim it on a new schedule with your vehicle’s VIN. For 2025, expect to total the interest yourself.

  1. Where: the new Schedule 1-A (“Additional Deductions”), Part IV, attached to Form 1040. Thomson Reuters
  2. VIN required: you enter the vehicle’s VIN on the return each year you claim it. RSM US
  3. No itemizing needed: it stacks on top of the standard deduction. IRS guidance

Form 1098-VLI (your lender’s statement)

Going forward, lenders that receive $600+ of qualifying interest from you must send Form 1098-VLI (“Vehicle Loan Interest Statement”) by January 31, reporting the interest and the VIN. IRS draft 1098-VLI

⚠
2025 is different. Under IRS Notice 2025-57, lenders are not required to file Form 1098-VLI for 2025 — they only have to make the interest total available to you. So for your 2025 return you may need to add up the interest yourself from your statements. Full 1098-VLI reporting starts with 2026. IRS transition relief

Records to keep

  • Lender interest statement (or 2025 equivalent / payoff history)
  • VIN + proof of U.S. assembly (NHTSA decoder result or window sticker)
  • Loan docs showing post-Dec-31-2024 origination, first lien, and personal use CALT
Check your eligibility → Estimate savings
Not tax advice. Educational information about the federal car-loan interest deduction, current as of June 2026. The IRS rules are proposed regulations and could change — verify with the IRS or a tax professional before filing.
Car Loan Interest Deduction

An independent, regularly-updated guide to the One Big Beautiful Bill Act auto-loan interest deduction (tax years 2025–2028). We are not affiliated with the IRS or any government agency.
